oatmeal-raisin-molasses-bread.name: Oatmeal Raisin Molasses Bread
instructions: Place oats, molasses, butter, and salt into the bread maker bowl; add the boiling water.
Measure 1/4 teaspoon sugar into a small cup and set aside. Pour remaining sugar into the oat mixture and stir. Let mixture cool to about 38°C/38 degrees C, about 20 minutes.
Pour warm water into the reserved sugar in the small cup; add yeast.
Pour bread flour atop the oat mixture. Pour yeast mixture over the flour.

Select Dough setting and press start. Check your owner's manual to see how long the kneading lasts, so you can add raisins to the dough 5 minutes before it finishes kneading. Let the bread maker run the full dough cycle, about 1 hour, though it will depend on your machine.
Grease 2 bread pans.

Turn dough out onto a lightly floured surface. Punch down dough, but do not knead. Separate into two loaves and place in prepared pans; cover each with a damp towel and allow to rise for about 1 hour.
Preheat oven to 177°C (175 degrees C).
Bake in preheated oven until the top is golden brown and the bottom of loaf sounds hollow when tapped, 15 to 20 minutes.

okra-in-the-oven-with-tomato-sauce-argir.name: Okra In The Oven With Tomato Sauce
okra-in-the-oven-with-tomato-sauce-argir.original_name: Μπάμιες στο φούρνο με σάλτσα ντομάτας
instructions: Wash the okra and let them drain completely from the water for a while.
Then clean them and salt them. Spread them into a large baking pan, sprinkle them with vinegar and mix.
Leave them in the sun or in a hot oven for one hour. To eliminate the sticky substance.
After this process, we drain them well in a colander to remove all the liquids.
We never wash them from vinegar and salt, because they will extract more sticky substance externally.
Cut the tomatoes into thick pieces, add them to a small pot and boil (without olive oil) over medium to high heat for 15 minutes, until they drink all their liquids and become almost like customers.
When the sauce is completely dry in the pot and has no liquids, add the olive oil.
Once warm, add the garlic cut into thin slices of sugar and some salt and pepper.
Lower the heat and simmer for 10 minutes. We watch the salt in the sauce because the okra is already salted.
Heat a large pan or pot on high heat, without adding olive oil, add the okra to the hot dry vessel and leave them for about 10 minutes, until completely dry, stirring regularly to help the process.
When they drink all their liquids, add 1⁄4 cup. olive oil and sauce the okra over medium to strong heat for 2-3 minutes.
Add the onion and sauce for 3-4 minutes to become transparent.
Add the paste and rub it with a wooden spoon at the base of the vessel for 1 minute, to bake and remove its flavors.

old-fashioned-ranch-teleculinaria.name: Old Fashioned Ranch
instructions: Wash and fix the chisper and ear. Lie in a pressure cooker, add the pork shovel, sausage and blond, season with salt and pepper and cover with water. Close the pan and take it to the fire, letting it cook for about 20 minutes since it starts boiling. Book the broth and cut the meat into pieces.

Light a pot with the olive oil and let it warm. Add the garlic and chopped onions and let it saute. Water with the wine and tomato pulp and let me see. Then add the broth to cook the meats and add water until 2 l of liquid. Let it boil.
Then add the dough and let it cook until it becomes soft. Add the grain, meat and sausage, mix and serve.

old-fashioned-porridge-and-molasses-brea.name: Old-fashioned Porridge And Molasses Bread

ingredients.item: Warm water (100 degrees f (38 degrees c))

instructions: Pour boiling water over oats and shortening in a bowl. Stir until shortening melts; let stand for 20 minutes, giving it a stir once in a while.
Meanwhile, dissolve sugar in lukewarm water. Add yeast and let stand for 10 minutes. Stir briskly with a fork.
Stir yeast mixture into the partially cooled oat mixture with molasses and salt. Let cool to lukewarm, 5 to 10 minutes. Beat in 2 1/2 cups of flour, gradually adding remaining flour as needed.

Turn dough onto a floured surface and knead, 8 to 10 minutes. Shape into a smooth ball and place in a greased bowl, rolling the dough so the top is greased. Cover and let double in size, about 1 1/2 hours.
Punch dough down and shape into 4 loaves. Place into greased 8 1/2x4 1/2-inch bread pans. Cover and let rise until doubled in size, about 1 hour.
Preheat the oven to 204°C (200 degrees C).
Bake in preheated oven until tops are golden brown, 30 to 35 minutes.

olive-oil-cake-recipe-lezzet.name: Olive Oil Cake Recipe

ingredients.item: 1 teaspoon orange or lemon peel grater

instructions: 1. Egg and sugar thoroughly frremove.
2. Add Milk and olive oil, mix.
3. Add and mix baking powder, flour and flavors.
4. Dökün to greased cake mold.
5. 170°C’de 35–40 minutes to cook.
6. slice the cold.

olive-oilpoached-trout.name: Olive Oil–poached Trout
instructions: Pour oil into a large skillet to come ¾" up sides and heat over medium until an instant-read thermometer registers 160°.
Carefully slip trout into oil, skin side down, and cook, adjusting heat to maintain temperature at 160°, until just opaque, about 3 minutes.
Using a fish spatula, transfer trout to a baking sheet, placing skin side up. Blot away excess oil with paper towels.
Squeeze lemon juice over both sides of each fillet, then season with salt. Serve with additional lemon wedges for squeezing over.

one-pan-garlic-parmesan-chicken-and-vege.name: One-pan Garlic Parmesan Chicken And Vegetable Bake
instructions: Cut broccoli and potatoes into small pieces and place on either side of a sheet tray.
Place two whole chicken breasts in the middle of the sheet tray. Drizzle the vegetables and chicken with olive oil (all but 1 tablespoon).
Drizzle the vegetables and chicken with olive oil (all but 1 tablespoon).
In a small bowl, blend the salt, pepper, onion powder, and paprika.
Evenly sprinkle the spice mixture over the veggies and both sides of the chicken. (TIP! Save any excess spice mix for next time.)
Preheat oven to 400˚F (200˚C).
Lightly mix the veggies on the pan with your hands to coat with the spices.
Rub the top of the chicken breast with crushed garlic, then sprinkle the parmesan cheese evenly over the chicken and vegetables.
Bake for 20-25 minutes, or until the chicken’s internal temperature reaches 165ºF (75ºC).
Enjoy!

onion-cheese-pie-argiro.name: Onion Cheese Pie
instructions: We make the homemade leaf first.
Add the flour and salt to the mixer's bowl with the knives (cutters).
Pour the cold butter into pieces and work the mixer for a minute.
The mixture will have become like wet sand.
Add 1 tablespoon of water and put the mixer on.
Add the next spoonful of water and put the mixer back on.
If necessary, add the remaining 1/2 tablespoon water, always with the mixer in operation.
When the dough is combined into a mass, gather it, wrap it in a membrane and leave it in the refrigerator for 15 minutes.
Heat the butter and sauté the opinions for about 10 minutes.
Sprinkle the nutmeg and let them cool.
Empty them in bowls.
Add the cheese, grated potato, eggs beaten, yogurt and cayenne.
Mix the filling well.
We bribe form or baking pan 25 cm.
Divide the dough to 2, one part a little larger.
Open the first leaf, spread it, empty the filling and cover with the second sheet.
We turn in the spare leaves.
We drill the pie here and there.
Bake in a preheated oven at 180.3.3. for about 45 minutes until the sheet is browned.

onion-tuna-traditional-cadiz-recipe-pequ.name: Onion Tuna (traditional Cadiz Recipe)

ingredients.item: 700 g of fresh tuna (if you can use bluefin tuna)
100 g aceite de oliva virgen extra

instructions: We put an extra 80 g virgin olive oil in a saucer and fry the tuna from both sides to get it golden. We put the tuna back to a deep plate, we'll put them back.
We add the remaining oil and onion that we will have cut into strips, garlic into sheets and bay leaf. We let them all stew at medium heat, till the onion's golden and tender about 30 minutes.

We remove a little onion into the beaker's glass and add water, we shredded everything and we put back to the casserole with the rest of the onion We put the tuna and let them cook for a few minutes. We have a taste test, if necessary we have to add some salt.

onions-stuffed-with-tuna-step-by-step-co.name: Onions Stuffed With Tuna Step By Step
onions-stuffed-with-tuna-step-by-step-co.original_name: Cebollas rellenas de atún paso a paso
instructions: He cuts his onion's base a little and removes some of his pulp that will be at the centre of his pilling.
Trocea la cebolla que cortaste hasta que quede finamente picada.
Peel his teeth with garlic and snap. Remove the seeds from the hot pepper and cut into small pieces.
Warm up a frying pan with some olive oil and spray your garlic and onion for a few minutes.
Incorporates and pits for another two minutes.
Add leaked preserved tuna, peeled tomatoes. Fitted with salt and pepper and thyme to taste. Meg and cook till you form a saffrit. That'll take about five minutes.
Preheat oven at 200 ° C
Filling onion with tuna and place onto a plate. Swallow the white wine and get to the oven.
Hornea for about 15 minutes at 180 ° C
Once soft, they'll be ready to enjoy.

orecchiette-with-kale-and-breadcrumbs.name: Orecchiette With Kale And Breadcrumbs

ingredients.item: Finely grated parmesan or grana padano

instructions: Working in batches, cook kale in a large pot of boiling salted water until just tender, about 4 minutes. Using tongs, transfer to a rimmed baking sheet; let cool. Set aside pot with water. Squeeze out excess liquid from kale; chop leaves and finely chop stems; set aside.

Heat 3 tablespoons oil in a small skillet over medium heat. Add breadcrumbs and cook, stirring often, until beginning to brown, about 4 minutes. Add one-third of chopped garlic and cook, stirring often, until breadcrumbs are golden, about 3 minutes. Season with salt and pepper and transfer to a paper towel-lined plate; let cool.

Heat butter and remaining 2 tablespoons oil in a large heavy pot over medium-low heat. Add anchovies, red pepper flakes, and remaining two-thirds of chopped garlic; cook, mashing anchovies with a spoon, until a paste forms, about 2 minutes. Add reserved kale and 1/2 cup water. Cook, stirring often, until kale is warmed through, about 4 minutes. Season with salt and pepper.
Meanwhile, bring reserved kale cooking liquid to a boil; add pasta and cook, stirring occasionally, until al dente. Drain, reserving 1 cup pasta cooking liquid.

Add pasta and 1/2 cup pasta cooking liquid to kale mixture and stir to coat. Increase heat to medium and continue stirring, adding more cooking liquid as needed, until sauce coats pasta. Mix in Parmesan and 1/2 cup breadcrumbs; toss to combine. Divide pasta among bowls, drizzle with oil, and top with remaining breadcrumbs.

oregano-eggs-uova-allorigano.name: Oregano Eggs (_uova All'origano_)
instructions: Set a large (12-inch) nonstick skillet over a burner that's still off. Swirl the pan with the olive oil, and gently break all the eggs to fill the pan, taking care not to break the yolks. Sprinkle with the salt and dried oregano. Sprinkle the grated cheese over all.
Cover the skillet, and turn the flame to medium-low. Cook until the whites are set and the yolks are done to your liking, about 7 to 8 minutes for still-runny yolks.
This is a great breakfast or main course, or, served over some arugula, it can be an appetizer.

oreo-cookie-sawdust-teleculinaria.name: Oreo Cookie Sawdust
instructions: Crush the cookies and book. Drain the gelatin leaves in cold water for 5 minutes. Drain the Jell-O and let it melt in the microwave without letting it boil.
Put the cream in a bowl, beat well, add the condensed milk and melted gelatine and wrap.
Distribute the cream by bowls and make layers of cream and crushed biscuits to look like a flower pot.
Take it to the fridge until it solidifies. Decorate with a flower and serve fresh.

oreo-cookies-and-cream-dessert.name: Oreo Cookies And Cream Dessert

ingredients.item: Container frozen whipped topping (such as cool whip®)

instructions: Combine cream cheese, powdered sugar, and butter in a large bowl; beat with an electric mixer until creamy. Add milk and vanilla pudding mix and beat until well combined and all lumps are gone. Fold in whipped topping.

Distribute 1/2 of the chocolate sandwich cookies on the bottom of a 9x13-inch dish. Cover with the pudding mixture and top with remaining cookie chunks. Cover and refrigerate for 8 hours to overnight.

osso-buco-recipe-with-mashed-sweet-potat.name: Osso Buco Recipe With Mashed Sweet Potato
osso-buco-recipe-with-mashed-sweet-potat.original_name: Receta de osobuco con puré de batata

ingredients.item: 2  piezas de osobuco de res (250-300 g cada una)
2 tazas de caldo de res casero o bajo en sodio
1 lemon plate (optional for glimolat at end)
1/3 taza de crema de leche o leche vegetal cremosa ( (como de coco o avena))

instructions: Dry up and scattering them generously.
In a deep pot or cocotte, heat olive oil and seal the osocoups from both sides until they are well golden. Withdraw and reserve.
In the same pot, sprinkle onion, garlic, carrot and celery, all finely minced. Cooking till tender and slightly caramelized.
Add the tomato purée, remove a few minutes and clear with the Red wine. Cooking till alcohol evaporate.
Regresa los osobucos a la olla, incorpora el caldo, las hierbas frescas y ajusta de sal y pimienta.
Cover and cook with very low heat for 2 to 2 and a half hours, or till the flesh takes off from the bone. Also can be made in oven at 160 been 3 been.
Wash and weigh the potatoes. They are cut into cubes and put into water with salt until they are very tender (15-20 minutes).
Listen to them and get them hot with butter, cream, nutmeg and a pint of salt. Bat till you get a soft and creamy purée. Taste suit.
Serves a pour pale bed and puts up his osocouple with his hot sauce. You can decorate with a touch of lime and fresh parsley to contrast flavors.

oven-roasted-chicken-with-grapes.name: Oven-roasted Chicken With Grapes

ingredients.item: Five-spice powder or  0.25 teaspoon ground cinnamon
Bunch red grapes on the stem

instructions: Preheat oven to 425°. Season chicken on both sides with five-spice powder, salt, and pepper.

Heat oil in a large ovenproof skillet over medium-high. Blot chicken with paper towels and add to skillet skin side down; add shallots. Cook chicken, undisturbed, until browned and crisp, about 5 minutes; turn shallots frequently to brown on all sides while chicken cooks. Turn breasts over and add thyme and grapes. Add vinegar and ¼ cup water to deglaze skillet. Season with salt and pepper.

Transfer skillet to oven and roast until chicken is cooked through and grapes just start to wrinkle, about 8 minutes. Serve chicken with roasted grapes and shallots alongside and any pan juices spooned over.

overnight-oats-with-cashews-seeds-and-tu.name: Overnight Oats With Cashews, Seeds, And Turmeric
instructions: Heat ghee in a small skillet over medium. Add cashews, pepitas, sunflower seeds, poppy seeds, fennel seeds, turmeric, and cayenne and cook, stirring constantly, until nuts are toasted and seeds are starting to pop, about 4 minutes. Remove from heat; season with salt.
Divide oats and yogurt among bowls, spooning yogurt into centers. Sprinkle nutritional yeast over, then top with cashew mixture (drizzle with any leftover ghee, if desired).
Do Ahead: Cashew mixture can be made 4 days ahead; cover and chill. Warm to re-liquefy ghee before serving.

oyster-stuffing-with-bacon-and-herbs.name: Oyster Stuffing With Bacon And Herbs

ingredients.item: Loaves italian or french bread (1 lb total)
Chopped fresh thyme or 1 tablespoon dried thyme
Finely chopped fresh sage or 2 teaspoons dried sage
Turkey giblet stock or low-sodium chicken broth

instructions: Preheat oven to 163°C.

Spread bread cubes in 2 shallow baking pans and bake in upper and lower thirds of oven, switching position of pans halfway through baking, until crispy and golden, 25 to 30 minutes total. Cool bread in pans on racks, then transfer to a large bowl.
Meanwhile, cook bacon in a 12-inch heavy skillet over moderate heat, stirring occasionally, until crisp, about 10 minutes. Transfer to paper towels to drain, reserving fat in skillet.

If bacon renders less than ¼ cup fat, add enough oil to skillet to total ¼ cup fat. Cook onions, celery, thyme, sage, garlic, salt, and pepper in fat in skillet over moderate heat, stirring occasionally, until vegetables are softened, 8 to 10 minutes. Transfer to bowl with bread cubes, then stir in bacon, parsley, butter, and oysters. Drizzle with stock, then season with salt and pepper and toss well.

Transfer bread mixture to a buttered 3- to 3½-quart shallow baking dish. Bake, covered, in middle of oven 30 minutes, then uncover and bake until browned, about 30 minutes more.
DO AHEAD: Stuffing can be assembled (without oysters and not baked) 2 days ahead and chilled, covered. Bring to room temperature and stir in oysters before baking.

oyster-tacos.name: Oyster Tacos
instructions: Place relish in a small fine-mesh sieve set over a small bowl; let drain for 10 minutes. Transfer relish to a medium bowl. Stir in mayonnaise and next 6 ingredients. Season with salt. Cover and chill. DO AHEAD: Can be made 1 day ahead. Keep chilled.

Whisk first 5 ingredients in a medium bowl. Attach deep-fry thermometer to side of a deep heavy pot. Pour oil into pot to a depth of 1". Heat oil over medium-high heat until deep-fry thermometer registers 350°–360°.
Working in batches, dredge oysters in cornmeal mixture and fry, stirring occasionally with a slotted spoon, until golden brown, about 2 minutes. Transfer to paper towels to drain.

Heat a griddle or a large skillet over medium-high heat. Working in batches, cook tortillas on griddle until softened and heated through, about 1 minute per side. Place 3 oysters in the center of each tortilla; top with some rémoulade, lettuce, and tomatoes.
